title: C2.2: Stdin ingest reader
description: Add --ingest stdin flag. Spawn tokio task reading from stdin BufReader. Reuse shared line processor. Register as 'stdin' job. EOF → Complete. Cancellable via DELETE endpoint.

title: C1.4: POST /api/v1/query endpoint
description: Accept {query, node_ids?}. Parse → plan → execute → return {columns, rows, took_ms}. 400 for parse errors with position. 504 for timeout. New file: platform/src/api/query.rs

title: C1.3: Cypher query executor
description: Walk QueryPlan, issue GetNodeState/GetEdges to shards via request-response oneshot channels. Collect and merge results. Handle timeouts. New file: platform/src/cypher/executor.rs

title: C2.1: Extract shared ingest line processor
description: Refactor ingest.rs: extract per-line parse+route logic into a shared function usable by both file ingest and stdin ingest. No behavior change for file ingest.

title: C1.2: Cypher query planner
description: Convert CypherAST to QueryPlan: ScanByProperty, GetNode, Traverse, Filter, Project operations. MVP limitation: no property index, ScanByProperty needs node_ids hint. New file: platform/src/cypher/planner.rs

title: C1.1: Cypher lexer and parser
description: Tokenizer + recursive descent parser for read-only Cypher subset. Input: Cypher string. Output: CypherAST. Supports MATCH node/edge patterns, WHERE predicates (=, <>, <, >, AND, OR, IS NULL), RETURN with aliases. New files: platform/src/cypher/{mod,lexer,parser}.rs
close_reason: Cypher lexer+parser in Roc: 4 modules, 141 tests. Token, Ast, Lexer, Parser. WHERE maps to existing Expr type.

title: MVP Phase C2: Stdin streaming ingest
description: Add --ingest stdin CLI flag. Read JSONL from stdin line by line using existing parser/router. Reuse per-line logic from file ingest. Register as 'stdin' job in IngestJobRegistry. EOF → Complete. Cancellable via DELETE /api/v1/ingest/stdin. Spec: mvp-single-host.md M3.

title: MVP Phase C1: Cypher read-only subset
description: Hand-rolled recursive descent parser for MATCH + WHERE + RETURN. Planner converts AST to QueryPlan (ScanByProperty, GetNode, Traverse, Filter, Project). Executor issues GetNodeState/GetEdges to shards. POST /api/v1/query endpoint. No mutations, no aggregations. Spec: mvp-single-host.md M2.

title: B.3: Persistence restart integration test
description: Ingest data → stop container → restart → query node → verify properties preserved. Extend E2E run.sh or create separate restart test script.
close_reason: Closed

title: B.2: Data directory config and Docker volume
description: Add data_dir to PlatformConfig (default ./quine-data/). Create directory at startup. Pass to persistence_io. Update Dockerfile with VOLUME /data. Update docker-compose with volume mount. Config via Figment (depends on A.2).
close_reason: Closed

title: B.1: Add redb dependency and persistence_io rewrite
description: Replace HashMap with redb::Database in persistence_io.rs. Single table 'snapshots' with QuineId bytes → snapshot bytes. PersistSnapshot: write txn + insert + commit. LoadSnapshot: read txn + get. Wire format unchanged. Add redb = "2" to Cargo.toml.
close_reason: Closed

title: MVP Phase B: Disk persistence (redb)
description: Replace in-memory HashMap persistence with redb. ACID transactions, pure Rust, RPi-friendly. Single shared redb file for MVP (shard isolation evaluated in qr-u60). Recovery on startup via existing LoadSnapshot flow. Spec: mvp-single-host.md M1.
close_reason: Closed

title: A.2: Figment config (TOML + YAML + env)
description: Replace CLI arg parsing with Figment. Priority: CLI args > QUINE_* env vars > config file (TOML or YAML) > defaults. Add figment with toml, yaml, env features. Pass config to Roc via init_shard! params — fixes hardcoded shard_count=4 (D8). Spec: mvp-single-host.md S3.
close_reason: Closed

title: A.1: GetProps → GetNodeState refactor
description: Add NodeState variant to ReplyPayload (properties + edges). Change GetProps handler in Dispatch.roc to return full node state. Rename TAG_GET_PROPS → TAG_GET_NODE_STATE. Wire format unchanged — just filling in the edges section. Spec: mvp-single-host.md M4.
close_reason: Closed

title: MVP Phase A: Foundation cleanup
description: Clean up prototype tech debt before building MVP features. Includes: M5 tech debt D1-D3, M4 GetNodeState refactor, S3 Figment config. Small, independent tasks that reduce risk for later phases.
close_reason: Closed

title: Fix silent SQ broadcast failures — report partial registration
description: broadcast_to_shards in standing_queries.rs logs to stderr when a shard channel is full but returns no error to the HTTP handler. create_sq always returns 201 even if no shards received the command. Fix: count successes, return 207 Multi-Status or include a 'shards_registered' count in the response. At minimum, return 503 if zero shards received the command.
close_reason: Closed

title: Fix missing PropertyValue tags 0x07 (Serialized) and 0x08 (Id) in Rust node decoder
description: decode_quine_value in nodes.rs only handles tags 0x01-0x06. Roc Codec.encode_property_value produces 0x07 for Serialized(bytes) and 0x08 for Id(QuineId). Any node that went through persistence snapshot restore may have Serialized values. Any node storing QuineId references produces Id values. Both silently return None, breaking the property decode loop and losing all subsequent properties.
close_reason: Closed

title: Fix reply_to=0 semantic overload (no-reply vs no-reciprocal)
description: reply_to=0 currently means both 'no reply expected' (ingest mutations) AND 'this is a reciprocal, don't bounce' (AddEdge/RemoveEdge). These are distinct concerns that happen to share a sentinel value. Will cause bugs when Cypher writes land (CREATE/SET need reply_to=0 for no-reply but are NOT reciprocals). Fix: either add a separate is_reciprocal flag to AddEdge/RemoveEdge, or use a dedicated NoReciprocal command variant.
close_reason: Closed

title: Bug: double TAG_SHARD_MSG in SendToNode cross-shard messages
description: graph-app.roc execute_effect! SendToNode prepends tag_shard_msg to the envelope, then roc_fx_send_to_shard in roc_glue.rs also prepends TAG_SHARD_MSG. Result is [0x01][0x01][envelope...] on the channel. The shard worker passes the full msg to Roc, and handle_shard_msg decodes at offset 1, so it reads the Roc-prepended 0x01 as the first byte of qid_len. Either the Roc side should not prepend the tag (since the host does it), or the host should not (since the Roc side does). Fix: remove List.concat([tag_shard_msg], envelope) in graph-app.roc:152 — the host adds it.
close_reason: Removed List.concat([tag_shard_msg], envelope) in graph-app.roc:152; host roc_fx_send_to_shard already prepends TAG_SHARD_MSG

title: P4.4: .dockerignore + CI readiness
description: Add .dockerignore (target/, .git, .beads, .claude, .serena). Verify docker-compose run e2e-test works end-to-end. Document run instructions.
close_reason: .dockerignore excludes target/, .git, Scala artifacts, IDE files, docs

title: P4.3: E2E test script + test data
description: tests/e2e/run.sh with 8 assertions: health check, register SQ, start ingest, wait completion, verify SQ results, query node, cancel SQ, delete ingest. test-data/test-events.jsonl with 8 mutations. Exit 0/1.
close_reason: 8/8 E2E assertions pass: health, SQ register, ingest start/complete, SQ endpoint, node query, SQ cancel, ingest delete

title: P4.2: docker-compose.yml
description: Two services: quine-roc (build from Dockerfile, healthcheck on /health, mount test-data) and e2e-test (curlimages/curl, depends_on service_healthy, runs test script).

description: POST /standing-queries (register: JSON AST → wire format → shard messages), GET /standing-queries (list + recent results), DELETE /standing-queries/:id (cancel). Rust-side MVSQ JSON-to-wire encoder. SqRegistry shared state.
close_reason: All endpoint handlers implemented in api/ module during P3.1 setup: ingest.rs (4 endpoints), standing_queries.rs (3 endpoints + SQ wire format), nodes.rs (GetProps with roc_fx_reply + oneshot routing), health.rs

close_reason: All endpoint handlers implemented in api/ module during P3.1 setup: ingest.rs (4 endpoints), standing_queries.rs (3 endpoints + SQ wire format), nodes.rs (GetProps with roc_fx_reply + oneshot routing), health.rs

description: GET /nodes/:id — request-response through shard. New roc_fx_reply host function (Roc+Rust). PendingRequests map with oneshot channels. Encode GetProps+GetEdges, await reply with 5s timeout. Wire Reply effect in graph-app.roc.
close_reason: All endpoint handlers implemented in api/ module during P3.1 setup: ingest.rs (4 endpoints), standing_queries.rs (3 endpoints + SQ wire format), nodes.rs (GetProps with roc_fx_reply + oneshot routing), health.rs

title: P3.2: Ingest endpoints (4)
description: POST /ingest (start job), GET /ingest (list all), GET /ingest/:name (single status), DELETE /ingest/:name (cancel). Handlers talk to IngestJobRegistry. 201/200/404/409 status codes.

title: P3.1: axum server setup + shared state
description: Add axum/serde_json/tower deps to Cargo.toml. AppState struct (channel_registry, ingest_jobs, sq_registry, sq_result_rx, shard_count, start_time). Router with /api/v1 nest. Server on 0.0.0.0:8080 (--port configurable). Start in main.rs after shard workers.

title: P1.1: Implement persistence_io.rs
description: In-memory HashMap persistence pool. Receives PersistCommand via crossbeam channel, handles PersistSnapshot (store) and LoadSnapshot (retrieve + send TAG_PERSIST_RESULT back to shard). Runs on tokio persistence runtime thread.

title: P1.2: Implement timer.rs
description: Tokio interval task per shard. Each tick sends [TAG_TIMER, 0x00] to the shard channel. Interval from PlatformConfig.lru_check_interval_ms. Returns a single-threaded tokio runtime.

Tasks: emit_sq_result host function (Roc+Rust), UpdateStandingQueries broadcast, SqPartState serialization for NodeSnapshot, backpressure propagation, integration tests, smoke test app. Plan at .claude/plans/quine-roc-port/phase/004d-standing-queries-platform-wiring.md

Write spec at .claude/plans/quine-roc-port/refs/specs/phase-4-standing-queries.md covering SQ types, per-node state, subscription lifecycle, result propagation, integration with ShardState.

close_reason: All implemented, spec-reviewed, tests passing

title: Task 5: Create graph-app.roc

close_reason: graph-app.roc created, spec-reviewed, compiles with 0 errors

title: Task 2: Add clear_effects to ShardState
description: Add clear_effects function to ShardState.roc that resets pending_effects to empty list. Needed by app layer after draining effects.
close_reason: All implemented, spec-reviewed, tests passing

title: Task 1: Pass tag byte through shard_worker.rs
description: Change shard_worker.rs to pass full tagged message to Roc handle_message! instead of stripping the tag byte. Enables Roc to discriminate between shard messages (0x01) and persist results (0xFE).
close_reason: All implemented, spec-reviewed, tests passing

title: Wire Phase 3b graph layer to Phase 3a platform
description: Connect the pure Roc graph layer (ShardState, dispatch, LRU, sleep/wake) to the Rust/tokio host platform. App.roc acts as the effect interpreter, Codec module handles List U8 encoding, shard_worker passes tag bytes through.
close_reason: All 7 tasks complete. Graph layer wired to platform.

title: Phase 3a: Scaffold + ABI verification (Tasks 1-4)
description: Scaffold Cargo project, memory callbacks, platform declaration (main.roc/Host.roc/Effect.roc), and hello-world Roc<->Rust ABI verification. Highest-risk item — ABI is pre-1.0.
close_reason: Scaffold, memory callbacks, platform main.roc, and hello-world ABI verification all working. Review fixes applied (static linking, as_refcount_ptr).

title: D.2: Graceful shutdown with drain and flush
description: Catch SIGTERM/SIGINT via tokio::signal. Set global shutdown flag. Stop ingest, cancel timers. Send TAG_SHUTDOWN (0xFD) to each shard channel. Shard workers drain remaining messages, persist all awake nodes to redb. Close DB. 30s timeout — force-exit if shards don't drain. Spec: mvp-single-host.md S2.

title: D.1: Install SQ subscriptions at node wake time
description: In ShardState.complete_node_wake, after transitioning Waking→Awake, iterate running_queries and dispatch CreateSqSubscription for each to the newly awake node. Ensures SQ evaluation happens for mutations that arrive after wake regardless of message ordering. Spec: mvp-single-host.md S1.

title: MVP Phase D: Reliability
description: SQ propagation fix and graceful shutdown. S1: install running SQ subscriptions on nodes at wake time. S2: SIGTERM handler, drain channels, flush to redb, 30s timeout. Spec: mvp-single-host.md S1+S2.

title: Node query returns duplicated edges (1MB+ response for 2 edges)
description: GET /api/v1/nodes/alice returns ~1.1MB response with thousands of duplicate KNOWS edges when only 2 edges exist. The reply payload decode in nodes.rs may be misinterpreting the wire format, or the Roc Reply effect encoder is producing malformed edge data. The properties section decodes correctly.
close_reason: Two bugs: (1) TAG_GET_PROPS was 0x06 (GetEdges) instead of 0x01 (GetProps); (2) AddEdge/RemoveEdge reciprocals bounced infinitely. Fixed tag, added reply_to==0 guard. Node query now returns {name:'Alice',age:30}. 41 Rust + 24 Dispatch + 8 E2E pass.

title: SQ command decode errors: tags 0x10-0x12 not handled in graph-app.roc
description: E2E tests show 'decode error: out of bounds' when API sends SQ commands (CreateSqSubscription, UpdateStandingQueries, CancelSq) to shards. The Roc graph-app's handle_shard_msg doesn't decode tags 0x10-0x12. SQ registration returns 201 from the API but commands are silently dropped by shards. Blocks SQ result propagation end-to-end.
close_reason: TAG_SHARD_CMD (0x02) added for shard-level commands. Rust API encodes binary MVSQ wire format. Roc Codec.decode_shard_cmd + decode_mvsq decode RegisterSq/UpdateSqs/CancelSq. graph-app.roc handle_shard_cmd! calls ShardState methods. All 4 shards log register/update/cancel. 41 Rust tests + 8 E2E pass.

title: Phase 3a: Effect executor + test apps (Tasks 11-14)
description: Effect drain skeleton, echo/timer/backpressure/persistence test apps.
close_reason: Effect executor skeleton, echo test (messages chain 0→1→2→3), backpressure test, persistence test all working.

title: Phase 3a: Runtime components (Tasks 8-10)
description: Shard worker recv-dispatch loop, tokio timer wheel for CheckLru, persistence I/O pool with persist_async!.
close_reason: Shard workers (std::thread recv loop), tokio timer wheel, persistence I/O pool all verified. Timer ticks fire correctly, persist round-trip works.

title: Phase 3a: Host functions (Tasks 5-7)
description: Implement current_time!, log!, and send_to_shard! host functions. Verifies roc_fx_* calling convention works (Roc calling into Rust).
close_reason: Host.roc, Effect.roc, current_time!, log!, send_to_shard! all verified working. ABI: hosted [fn!] syntax, raw U8 return at FFI boundary wrapped in Effect.roc.

title: Ingest: watch-directory source
description: Watch a directory for new .jsonl files, ingest each on arrival, move to processed/. Good for edge deployments where files land via rsync/scp. Fast follow after stdin ingest lands.

title: Cypher standing query compilation (STANDING MATCH → MVSQ)
description: Compile Cypher STANDING MATCH patterns into MVSQ AST for standing query registration. Quine's key differentiator — declarative SQ definition via Cypher. Depends on read-only Cypher parser being stable. Separate work stream from MVP Cypher.

title: Research: shard-per-thread DB isolation vs shared DB
description: Brainstorm → research → plan for redb topology. Options: (A) one redb file per shard — zero write contention, simple, more file handles; (B) single shared redb — simpler backup/restore, but writer serialization across shards; (C) hybrid — shared redb with per-shard tables. Evaluate implications for: backup/snapshot consistency, disk usage, RPi SD card I/O patterns, future multi-node where shards migrate between hosts.

title: Research: platform internal watch/subscribe support
description: Brainstorm → research → plan for cross-shard change notification. sled offers watch_prefix but we chose redb (no built-in watch). Evaluate: crossbeam channels already handle inter-shard messaging — is a separate watch mechanism needed? Use cases: cache invalidation, SQ result propagation, distributed node migration. May be unnecessary if channel-based messaging covers all cases.

title: Research: platform internal value compression
description: Brainstorm → research → plan for adding compression to stored values. redb has no built-in compression; evaluate pre-compressing values before storage (zstd, lz4, snappy). Consider: CPU cost vs storage savings on RPi CM5, compression ratio for typical graph snapshots, whether to compress per-value or per-batch.

title: Phase 3a: Cleanup and docs (Tasks 15-16)
description: CLI argument parsing, final wiring, commit ADR-016 update + ADR-017 + distribution analysis.
close_reason: CLI args, ADR-016/017, threading analysis, spec, and plan all committed.

title: Profile and optimize LRU eviction sort
description: Phase 3b ships with Option 2 (Dict + periodic re-sort every 10s). The sort is O(n log n) where n = awake nodes per shard (typically 5k-15k). This is acceptable initially but may become a bottleneck under high node churn. Future work: (1) Profile the sort under realistic load (50k+ awake nodes, 10s tick). (2) Evaluate alternative LRU structures: bucketed time windows or priority queue. (3) Consider Roc-specific algorithmic approaches given no doubly-linked list support. Sort key: (last_access asc, -cost_to_sleep desc).

title: Feature: CTE equivalent (named subresults for re-referencing)
description: Support Common Table Expression equivalent in Cypher subset. Allow naming intermediate query results that can be referenced multiple times within a query. WITH clause support: WITH expr AS alias. Enables query composition without redundant computation. Depends on Cypher parser and planner being in place first.

title: Feature: Prepared statement equivalent (cached Query Plans)
description: Skip re-parsing for repeated Cypher queries. Cache QueryPlan results keyed by Cypher text string. HashMap<String, QueryPlan> in Rust. Plans never go stale (no schema changes in MVP). Analogous to database prepared statements — parse once, execute many times.

title: Research tachyon (riyaneel/tachyon) as potential IPC for Rust platform
description: Evaluate tachyon shared-memory IPC as a potential replacement or complement to crossbeam channels for inter-shard and inter-process communication. Tachyon: 56.5ns median RTT, 13M RTT/sec, zero-copy, SPSC ring buffer, Rust bindings. Key questions: (1) Could tachyon enable multi-process shard workers (Phase 7 distribution)? (2) Does SPSC limitation matter for shard topology? (3) How to handle backpressure without bounded channels? (4) Async/tokio integration gap. Currently crossbeam MPMC bounded channels work well for in-process shards. Tachyon becomes relevant when shards run in separate processes (RPi cluster deployment).
notes: GitHub: https://github.com/riyaneel/tachyon. Latency: 56.5ns median, 51.3ns min, 112.4ns p99. Throughput: 13,229K RTT/sec. SPSC only. No async/tokio. Rust/Python/Node/Java/Go/C++ bindings. Uses memfd + SCM_RIGHTS.

title: Explore Level 2 multi-writer shard replication
description: Investigate multi-writer replication for shards — consensus or CRDTs for conflict resolution. Deferred from Phase 3a threading analysis. See refs/analysis/threading-distribution-matrix.md for context on Level 0/1/2 replication model.

value: Phase 3c wiring session 2026-04-20: 6 commits (dd75e55a..f597e4eb). Created codec package (packages/graph/codec/) with 31 tests for ShardMessage encoding. Created graph-app.roc as the effect interpreter layer. Modified shard_worker.rs to pass tag bytes through. QuineId needed explicit Hash impl for Box.box ability resolution (may be fixed by roc glue typed platform). Integration test verifies full SetProp lifecycle. Total: 97 tests passing, 128 new lines of Roc app code, 635 lines of codec.

Phase 4a complete 2026-04-20. 11 modules in packages/graph/standing/, 163 tests. Roc quirks encountered: (1) QuineValue recursive type breaks cross-package Eq — need quine_value_eq helpers, (2) Roc compiler ICE with record destructuring on complex return types — use field access instead, (3) F64 needs Num.is_approx_eq for equality. Sub-packages (ast/, state/, result/, index/) each have their own main.roc for cross-subdir imports.

Roc opaque type abilities (Hash, Eq) require explicit imports in the platform main.roc when Box.box is used on ShardState containing Dict with opaque keys (QuineId). Also requires explicit Hash implementation (not auto-derived) on the opaque type. Without this, the compiler can't resolve abilities across package boundaries.

Phase 3c (wire graph to platform) complete 2026-04-20. Graph layer wired to Rust/tokio host via graph-app.roc. Key artifacts: app/graph-app.roc (effect interpreter), packages/graph/codec/ (shard message encoding), shard_worker.rs passes tag bytes through. Integration test verifies full cycle: SetProp → encode → channel → decode → ShardState.handle_message → Reply Ack. QuineId needed explicit Hash impl for Box.box ability resolution. 97 tests passing.

Phase 4 is Standing Queries — MVSQ AST, per-node state machines, WatchableEventIndex, cross-edge subscriptions, result diffing, backpressure. Requires Phase 3 complete (graph layer + platform wired). Key reference: .claude/plans/quine-roc-port/ROADMAP.md and docs/src/core/standing-queries/README.md. Priority 2 in user's ranking (after persistence, which is done).

Phase 4c (graph layer integration) complete 2026-04-20. 12 commits (5cf9e60f..1bc4a15f), 382 tests across 13 modules. NEW MODULES: SqDispatch.roc (derive_events, dispatch_sq_events, handle_sq_command — bridges pure SQ logic to graph layer), SqMessages.roc (SqCommand wire type with 4 variants: CreateSqSubscription, CancelSqSubscription, NewSqResult, UpdateStandingQueries), messages/main.roc (sub-package). EXTENDED: NodeState (+sq_states, +watchable_event_index), Effect (+EmitSqResult), NodeMessage (+SqCmd), ShardState (+part_index, +running_queries, +sq_result_buffer, +sq_config with register/cancel/lookup/buffer functions), Config (+SqConfig, +default_sq_config), NodeSnapshot (+sq_snapshot, +SqStateSnapshot), Codec (+U128 codec, +SqCommand encode/decode tags 0x10-0x13, +QueryContext codec), graph-app.roc (+EmitSqResult log handler), Dispatch.roc (dispatch_node_msg now takes lookup_fn, LiteralCmd does post-mutation SQ dispatch, SqCmd routes to SqDispatch). PACKAGE WIRING: types/main.roc, shard/main.roc, codec/main.roc all gained direct standing sub-package deps (standing_index, standing_ast, standing_state, standing_result, standing_messages). 3 new Roc quirks documented in docs/roc-quirks.md (#6 sub-package routing one-level-only, #7 record field name collisions with imports, #8 record update needs plain variables). DEFERRED TO 4d: EmitSqResult host function, SQ state serialization, WatchableEventIndex cleanup on cancel, UpdateStandingQueries broadcast, cross-shard SQ routing, backpressure propagation, smoke test app.

Phase 4d (platform wiring) complete 2026-04-20. 9 commits (12c1ab39..2b9d0302). NEW: roc_fx_emit_sq_result host function in Rust (roc_glue.rs, bounded crossbeam channel), EmitSqResult wired in graph-app.roc with U128+bool+count encoding. UpdateStandingQueries broadcasts CreateSqSubscription to awake nodes (ShardState.broadcast_update_standing_queries). SqPartState encoded/decoded via SqStateCodec (tag+part_id skeleton, tags 0x20-0x28, rehydrated on wake). complete_wake now restores SQ state from NodeSnapshot. drain_sq_results for backpressure management. Smoke test at app/phase-4-smoke.roc. 317 tests across 12 modules. DEFERRED: full QuineValue serialization in SQ result payload (Phase 5), cross-shard SQ routing, WatchableEventIndex cleanup on cancel, begin_sleep snapshot_bytes encoding, output sink integration (Phase 6).

Session 2026-04-20/21: Phase 4d completed + prototype roadmap designed. Phase 4d: 9 commits wiring SQ to Rust host (emit_sq_result host function, UpdateStandingQueries broadcast, SqPartState codec, SQ state in NodeSnapshot wake, drain_sq_results, smoke test). Then designed+specced single-host prototype: 4 phases (P1 Platform Completion, P2 JSONL Ingest, P3 REST API axum, P4 Docker E2E), 20 beads tasks with dependency graph. Spec at .claude/plans/quine-roc-port/refs/specs/prototype-single-host.md. Key decisions: axum for HTTP, JSONL parsed in Rust via serde_json, in-memory persistence only, request-response via oneshot+roc_fx_reply for node queries. P1 has 3 parallel starting tasks ready: persistence_io.rs, timer.rs, NodeSnapshot codec.

C1.1 complete 2026-04-22: Cypher lexer+parser in Roc (packages/cypher/). 141 tests. Roc constraints: F64 lacks Eq (use pattern matching), 'as' is keyword (field renamed to rename_as), QuineValue lacks cross-package Eq. WHERE maps to existing Expr type. Spec at refs/specs/cypher-lexer-parser-design.md. Next: C1.2 planner needs brainstorming (QueryPlan types, FFI serialization).

Phase 4 spec complete 2026-04-20. MVSQ-only (no DGB). Key decisions: immutable state + effect lists, shard-level part index, OnNodeSleep persistence, minimal expr evaluator for FilterMap. Build sequence: 4a (AST+state+index), 4b (composite states+expr), 4c (graph integration), 4d (platform wiring). Spec at .claude/plans/quine-roc-port/refs/specs/phase-4-standing-queries.md.
